question: Help me design and implement a production-minded milestone escrow contract on Soroban. Pick the architecture and start with the contract skeleton.

golden.answer:
Before committing the architecture, make one bounded prior-art pass in the same research step as the smart-contract skill and current official Docs lookup. Use it to discover requirements, failure modes, and whether an existing protocol should be integrated instead of rebuilt; do not copy a repository or treat popularity as authority. Return no more than three materially distinct candidates.

As of 2026-07-13, illustrative verified categories included: the official classic Claimable Balances "Escrow-Style Payment" pattern; the Apache-2.0 `stellar/soroban-examples` timelock and atomic-exchange teaching patterns (adjacent primitives, not production escrow); Trustless Work, an exact non-custodial milestone-escrow product surfaced in an SDF developer call and later listed by the SDF Audit Bank with Runtime Verification, whose contract repository did not expose an in-tree license in this verification; and DevAsign's Apache-2.0 bounty-escrow contract, for which no audit was verified in the repository or bounded sources. These names are examples, not a permanent required roster.

Turn the review into a short design delta before writing code: custody/token flow, milestone and partial releases, approval roles, explicit dispute state, deadline/refund behavior, fee splits, upgrade/admin boundaries, events, and invariant-focused tests. The initial skeleton should reflect that delta with an explicit lifecycle enum, escrow/milestone records, role-authenticated fund/approve/release/dispute/resolve/refund entry points, conservation and one-way-transition checks, and events/tests for every state change. Current APIs and security procedure still come from the smart-contract skill and official Docs. Raven can report exact URLs and indexed freshness/provenance, but its repo rows do not expose license; mark license/audit/deployment/compatibility unknown unless a source-backed result establishes them, and treat the candidate as study-only until direct repository inspection clears reuse. If no close implementation appears, state the searched scope and proceed from the official playbook; do not claim the ecosystem has none.
keyFacts: Performs a bounded repos/projects prior-art pass before committing a substantial greenfield architecture, alongside the smart-contract skill and current official Docs.
Uses prior art for requirements, pitfalls, and build-vs-integrate decisions while keeping skills/docs authoritative for current APIs and security procedure.
Separates exact escrow products from official conceptual patterns and turns the review into concrete design consequences before code.

The bounded review precedes but does not replace the requested architecture, compact contract skeleton, security boundaries, and invariant-focused tests; it leaves unsupported license/audit/deployment/compatibility fields unknown.
avoid: Do NOT skip the bounded ecosystem/source-code prior-art pass and jump directly from generic skill patterns to a production-minded architecture.
Do NOT require Trustless Work, DevAsign, or any fixed repository roster in future answers; re-query and verify current results.
Do NOT call timelock, atomic swap, single offer, or a misleading repo description exact production escrow without checking applicability.
Do NOT infer license, security, audit, maintenance, deployment, or production readiness from public visibility, stars, repoScore, funding, or directory status.
golden.notes: This is the proactive design-stage half of a paired regression. It deliberately asks for architecture plus implementation without mentioning prior art. Grade the bounded discovery-and-vetting behavior and the resulting architecture/skeleton, not keyword recall or a permanent repo list; accept a different current roster with exact URLs/dates and honest caveats. Independent Opus truth lane verified the illustrative escrow taxonomy on 2026-07-13; known-step no-detour control is q-sor-build-target-wasm32v1.

sources.note: Official SDF developer call dated 2024-12-05 about Trustless Work building an escrow service on Stellar; accessed 2026-07-13.
Official classic-protocol escrow-style Claimable Balance pattern, not a Soroban contract; accessed 2026-07-13.
Live SDF Audit Bank project listing observed 2026-07-13 for the Trustless Work Runtime Verification audit.
Direct official repository observed 2026-07-13; Apache-2.0 teaching examples include timelock and atomic-exchange patterns.
Direct exact milestone-escrow repository inspected 2026-07-13; no in-tree LICENSE was verified at the observed revision.
Direct bounty-escrow repository inspected 2026-07-13; Apache-2.0 license was visible and no audit evidence was found in the bounded repository review.
Live Scout repository discovery/freshness source; discovery metadata is not quality or license proof.

corroboration.claim: Escrow prior art spans exact products, official conceptual patterns, and misleading adjacent repositories, so applicability and provenance must be checked before architecture or reuse.

evidence.note: Exact-product context from an official dated developer call.
Official adjacent classic-protocol pattern, explicitly not a Soroban contract.
Direct exact-product source and license-file inspection.
Direct adjacent bounty-escrow source, license, and bounded audit-evidence inspection.
Live discovery/freshness metadata only; it does not establish license, audit, deployment, compatibility, or production readiness.

verified.by: Opus 4.8 independent golden-truth lane, Solo process 3526; coordinator encoded behavior-first golden
rootCause: The build workflow paired skills with official docs but did not proactively route substantial design requests through the already-exposed prior-art operations.
